name: Pizza Dough & Variations
recipeIngredient: 3 1/4 cups bread, semolina, or unbleached all-purpose flour
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the sugar in warm tap water at 110-115°F.
2. Sprinkle yeast over the dissolved sugar and stir gently until it dissolves (1 minute).
3. Let stand in a warm spot until a thin layer of foam covers the surface, about 5 minutes. Discard mixture if no bubbles form within 5 minutes.
4. If making whole-wheat dough, combine the flours as specified.
5. If making cornmeal dough, combine the flour and cornmeal as specified.
6. Combine 3 cups of flour with salt in a large mixing bowl.
7. Make a well in the center and pour in yeast mixture and oil (if using).
8. Stir vigorously to incorporate the flour into the well, starting from the center and working toward the sides.
9. Gradually add more flour until the dough is no longer sticky or tacky. Knead gently until smooth, elastic, and springy.
10. Shape the dough into a ball, cover with oil, and let rise in a warm place (75-85°F) until doubled in bulk (45 minutes for quick yeast to 1-1.5 hours for regular yeast).
11. Punch down the dough and shape it again, removing air bubbles.
12. If using bread flour or semolina flour, turn the dough in an oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate until puffy (35 minutes to 1 hour). Omit if using all-purpose flour.
13. Refrigerated dough can be punched down and used up to 4 times within 36 hours. Let it come to room temperature before proceeding.
